replication_study.hypothesis: The average market-clearing price (group-round mean market price) in the experimental sessions is significantly different from the fixed price of 1.94 (two-tailed); equivalently, Uniform vs Fixed treatment leads to different average market prices when compared to 1.94 as in the original analysis.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Lab experiment raw session exports: round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv (located in replication_data/). Derived analysis files: round1_analysis.csv and fullsample_analysis.csv.
data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment session exports (CSV) from oTree/experiment platform
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Round1 raw (round1_raw.csv) and round2 raw (round2_raw.csv) will be combined/processed as in replication_analysis.do; round1-only analysis will also be reported.
data_plan.sample_size: See derived files: round1_analysis.csv contains 21 group-level observations; fullsample_analysis.csv contains 40 group-level observations (these are the group-level summaries produced by the supplied .do and CSVs). The raw datasets have 240 and 400 rows respectively (participant-round rows) — final sample size for group-level tests equals number of unique groups after filtering (reported in derived CSVs).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Group-round (collapsed group average market price) and group-level means (groups aggregated across rounds). The original used group-round outcomes and aggregated group means per treatment.
data_plan.access_details: Files included in the repository under data/original/27/input/replication_data: round1_raw.csv, round2_raw.csv, round1_analysis.csv, fullsample_analysis.csv. No external access restrictions indicated in provided files.

qualification.explanation: The provided raw CSVs are direct session exports from the same type of laboratory experiment described in the paper. They include per-round group market prices and group identifiers, enabling the same summary measures and Wilcoxon signed-rank tests used in the original paper. The .do script included with replication_data expresses identical processing logic to what the paper reports (reshape price columns for rounds 1-20, compute group-round means, create dropout/bankrupt indicators, run signrank tests vs 1.94).
qualification.similarity_to_original: High fidelity: The experimental structure and variables used in the analysis are present. Evidence: post_registration.json documents the original experiment design and focal outcome (seller revenue/market price) and analysis steps (nonparametric signrank tests). The raw CSV headers include 'ipo_task.<r>.group.market_price' variables and group-level columns (seen in replication_data/round1_raw.csv — column listing shows ipo_task.1.group.market_price ... ipo_task.20.group.market_price). The original claim in initial_details.txt explicitly references the two-tailed Wilcoxon signed-ranks test comparing average prices to 1.94 (initial_details.txt: "Wilcoxon signed-ranks test ... p = 0.001").
qualification.deviation_from_original: Minor differences / notes: post_registration.json reports analysis based on 28 valid groups and ~280 rounds (paper's stated sample). The provided analysis CSVs in replication_data (fullsample_analysis.csv has 40 rows, round1_analysis.csv has 21 rows) indicate the available sample may differ in group counts compared to the paper's reported 28 groups — the data provided appear to include different session counts or additional groups. This constitutes a different sample (new instance) but the design and variables match the original design (so replication tests robustness rather than reproducing an identical sample). References: post_registration.json (original sample description) vs data/original/27/input/replication_data/fullsample_analysis.csv (observed group counts).

data_plan.notes: Key variables present: ipo_task.<r>.group.market_price for r=1..20, ipo_task.1.group.id_in_subsession (group id), ipo_task.1.group.task_type (treatment: 'UNIFORM' / 'FIXED'), and indicators enabling dropout/bankrupt flags (rounds_missed variables). The provided Stata .do uses relative paths like .\round1_raw.csv; in our replication environment ensure paths point to /app/data/..., and that the analysis script reads these CSVs from /app/data/data/original/27/input/replication_data/ (or mount ./data as /app/data). Potential caveat: small differences in group counts between post_registration.json and fullsample_analysis.csv; ensure documented when interpreting differences in p-values. Also be aware of missing values in some rounds which are handled by the original .do (dropout, bankrupt flags).

steps: 1) Load round1_raw.csv into pandas and filter to groups with ipo_task.1.group.id_in_subsession > 1 (to drop session-control rows as in original .do).
2) Rename the per-round market price columns ipo_task.<r>.group.market_price to price1...price20, and identify the rounds_missed variable (ipo_task.20playertotal_missing_re or similar).
3) Reshape the dataset from wide to long so that each row is a group-round with columns: group id, session.code, round number, price, rounds_missed.

4) Create a group identifier (equivalent to Stata egen group(session-level group id, session.code)), then aggregate/collapse to compute group-round mean price, rounds_missed_max (max per group-round), rounds_missed_min.
5) Generate dropout indicator (rounds_missed_max >= 6) and bankrupt indicator (rounds_missed_min == -99) following .do logic.

6) Run Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (two-tailed) comparing price to fixed value 1.94 on three subsets: all observations, observations with dropout==0, and observations with dropout==0 & bankrupt==0. (This mirrors signrank price=1.94; signrank price=1.94 if !dropout; signrank price=1.94 if !dropout&!bankrupt in the .do).
7) Collapse to group-level means (mean price per group across rounds) and export round1_analysis.csv and fullsample_analysis.csv as in the original .do outputs.

8) Report test statistics and p-values, and compare them to the originally reported p = 0.001 and direction of effect (UNIFORM higher than FIXED). Optionally compute treatment-stratified summaries by subsetting on ipo_task.1.group.task_type == 'UNIFORM' vs 'FIXED' and comparing group means across treatment using nonparametric tests (signed-rank or rank-sum as appropriate).
planned_method.models: Nonparametric tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank tests for one-sample comparison to fixed price 1.94; rank-sum/Mann-Whitney tests or summary comparisons if doing between-treatment comparisons), descriptive group-round mean calculations.
planned_method.outcome_variable: price (market-clearing price) — group-round mean price (and group-level mean price aggregated across rounds). Specifically created from ipo_task.<r>.group.market_price variables and the subsequent collapse to group-round and group-level means.
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: treatment indicator (ipo_task.1.group.task_type: 'UNIFORM' vs 'FIXED') where used for between-treatment comparisons. For the original focal claim, the test is price vs fixed price 1.94 (one-sample test) so the comparison is against the fixed price value.
planned_method.control_variables: None used in the original nonparametric tests. Dropout and bankrupt indicators are used to subset the sample (to report robustness). If regression extensions are added, group composition or round number could be included.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10+; recommended libraries: pandas, numpy, scipy (for wilcoxon), statsmodels. The provided original analysis uses Stata (.do) but we translate the logic to Python. Execution environment should mount the repository data folder at /app/data.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Estimate group-round mean price and group-level mean price; compute Wilcoxon signed-rank statistic for (price - 1.94).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wilcoxon signed-rank test (two-tailed) comparing observed price to 1.94. For between-treatment comparisons, Mann-Whitney U test / rank-sum may be used as appropriate.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Follow original .do: create dropout indicator (rounds_missed_max >= 6) and bankrupt indicator (rounds_missed_min == -99), report tests on full sample and subsets excluding dropouts and excluding both dropouts & bankrupt groups. Otherwise use listwise deletion for missing price entries (drop NA prices before tests).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for the focal one-sample tests; if multiple outcomes are tested we will report unadjusted p-values and note multiple-testing (and optionally apply Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg correction for exploratory multiple comparisons).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary significance threshold p < 0.05 (two-tailed). Compare direction of effect to original finding (reported p = 0.001 and direction UNIFORM > FIXED for average price). Report exact p-values and Wilcoxon test statistics.

files.replication_analysis__py.py: Python translation of replication_analysis.do. Intended behavior: read raw CSVs (round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv) from /app/data/data/original/27/input/replication_data/, reshape price columns (price1..price20), create group identifiers and dropout/bankrupt flags, compute group-round means and group-level means, run Wilcoxon signed-rank tests comparing price to 1.94 for (a) all observations, (b) non-dropouts, (c) non-dropouts & non-bankrupt. Save outputs to fullsample_analysis.csv and round1_analysis.csv in the same replication_data folder. Uses pandas, numpy, scipy.stats.wilcoxon. (Note: the original repository contains replication_analysis.do; this file is the Python translation of that logic.)
files.run_replication.py: Small runner script that executes replication_analysis__py.py (wrapper). Use this to set working directory and call the analysis script in the replication_data folder if desired.
files.replication_analysis.do: The original Stata .do script included in replication_data. Kept as reference; it performs identical transformations/tests in Stata. Used to verify translation fidelity.
files.round1_raw.csv, round2_raw.csv: Raw data files (oTree/experiment exports) used as inputs by the analysis scripts. They contain per-round group market prices (ipo_task.<r>.group.market_price) and group-level task_type (treatment).
files.round1_analysis.csv, fullsample_analysis: Derived outputs that the original .do exports; included in the repository and useful as immediate checks of the replication results.

codebase.notes: I translated the logic from replication_analysis.do into Python (replication_analysis__py.py). The Python script should be executed from the replication_data directory, and it reads/writes files under /app/data/data/original/27/input/replication_data/ (ensure host mounts ./data as /app/data). Dependencies: pandas, numpy, scipy. If running inside Docker/container, ensure the working directory is set so relative imports and file paths resolve correctly. The original .do uses relative Windows-style paths (".\round1_raw.csv"); the Python script uses absolute /app/data/ path to avoid ambiguity. If you prefer, set current working directory to replication_data and run the script with local relative paths.

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure the repository data directory is mounted to /app/data. 2) From the replication_data directory (data/original/27/input/replication_data), run: python replication_analysis__py.py  (or run run_replication.py which invokes replication_analysis__py.py). 3) The script will read round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv, produce group-round aggregates, compute dropout/bankrupt indicators, run Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (price vs 1.94) on full sample and subsets, and write round1_analysis.csv and fullsample_analysis.csv to the same folder. 4) Inspect printed Wilcoxon statistics and p-values in stdout and compare to the original reported p=0.001; also compare the group-level mean prices to the originals. 5) For between-treatment summaries, compute group-level means split by ipo_task.1.group.task_type == 'UNIFORM' vs 'FIXED' and run a two-sample nonparametric test (Mann-Whitney U) or report descriptive comparisons.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Primary: Wilcoxon signed-rank statistic and p-value (price vs 1.94). Secondary: group-level mean prices (means and standard deviations) and directionality (whether means are higher than 1.94 and whether UNIFORM > FIXED). If available, also report Mann-Whitney U p-values for treatment differences and Spearman correlation between market price and market value (as in original). Report sample sizes (number of group-round observations and number of groups) for transparency.
